name: John Hickenlooper
subject: Cutting through the noise
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hickenlooper 2020
body:
This race is crowded, and the other candidates are armed with promises that offer more style than substance--which make for slick soundbites, but not for real strategies to move our country forward. | | | [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/6377170b/691c9bd/6e8dbba2/11a45436/2963509739/VEsH/?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_template")
---
| Friend,

Yesterday, I got to share my vision for America on stage in the second Democratic debates of this election.

As of tonight, I've also had the opportunity to hear from 19 fellow candidates for President of the United States. And over the past two days, one thing has become very clear--**I am the only candidate with proven experience reaching across the aisle and implementing progressive policies.**

But this race is crowded, and the other candidates are armed with promises that offer more style than substance--which make for slick soundbites, but not for real strategies to move our country forward.

I know that I have the experience to do this job better than anyone on that debate stage. I've run a business, a city and a state. But experience alone won't be enough to break through the noise.

**That 's why I need your help. The last 48 hours have been a whirlwind--but it's the next 4 hours that will be critical for this campaign.**

[**_Our monthly fundraising deadline is at midnight tonight, and we need to show the country just how strong our team is._**](https://secure.hickenlooper.?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_fr_20190731_JH_20190731-fr-JH-noise-all_all_l1&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=&zip=34223&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

The success of our pragmatic, progressive vision is on the line. The money we raise tonight will impact how many voters I can reach, how many states I can travel to, and if I qualify for the next debate.

**If we all come together tonight, I _will_ be a leader in the next, pivotal phase of the 2020 race.** [**_Can you chip in before 12 AM ET to get me there?_**](https://secure.hickenlooper.?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_fr_20190731_JH_20190731-fr-JH-noise-all_all_l2&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=&zip=34223&phone=&occupation=&employer=") [](https://secure.hickenlooper.?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_fr_20190731_JH_20190731-fr-JH-noise-all_all_contribbutton&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=&zip=34223&phone=&occupation=&employer=") Giddy Up,
John

name: Addisu Demissie, Cory 2020
subject: "The person enjoying this debate the most is Donald Trump."
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cory 2020
body:
Team, I'm backstage at the DNC Detroit debate right now and I thought it'd be a good time to check in.
Cory's made it clear tonight: "The person enjoying this debate the most is Donald Trump. As we pick each other apart, the Republicans are trying to gut Obamacare."
**Again and again, Donald Trump has shown us who he is. The question in this election is: who are we as a people? But Cory has a plan.**
America has serious problems, deep wounds, and deeply-rooted injustices.
Our call in this election is to unite in common cause and common purpose. That's how Cory will beat Donald Trump and that's how he'll lead our party and our country as your president.
**[If you like what you're hearing from Cory on that stage, can you rush a donation by midnight so we can meet our end-of-month fundraising deadline?](https://act.corybooker.?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190731-em4?refcode=em_fr_20190731_20190731-fr-AD-middebate-actives_actives_l1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")**
When he walks off that stage, I would love to be able to tell him that a huge number of small-dollar donors stepped up tonight to help us smash our July fundraising goal.
What do you say, team? Will you help make sure that I'm able to tell Cory we hit our goal when the debate is over?
**We 're $14,267 away from our $400,000 July goal. Chip in here: [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190731-em4](https://act.corybooker.?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190731-em4?refcode=em_fr_20190731_20190731-fr-AD-middebate-actives_actives_l2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")**
Alright, that's enough from me. We've still got some more time left here in Detroit -- so tune in if you haven't already and enjoy the rest of the debate!
Talk soon,
Addisu
Addisu Demissie
Campaign Manager
Cory 2020

| Friend -

Michael just made clear on the debate stage that our education system is reinforcing the inequality we face in this country, not liberating people from it.

He drew a direct line from slavery and Jim Crow to red-lining and mass incarceration.

Our schools are more segregated than they were 50 years ago.

The majority of people in prison dropped out of high school.

One group of kids has access to pre-school and great K-12 schools, and another does not.

name: Wesley Hunt
subject: We Must Stand Up
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hunt for Congress
body:
The past 2 nights should worry all Americans. If it wasn't clear before, the Democratic presidential debates are proof that the Democratic Party is t
Dear Friend,

The past 2 nights should worry all Americans.

If it wasn't clear before, the Democratic presidential debates are proof that the Democratic Party is the party of Bernie Sanders, Elizabeth Warren and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, **not** the party of middle-class Texans.

They care more about decriminalizing illegal immigration, bankrupting the energy industry and taking away your private insurance than advocating for cities like Houston.

We MUST stand up to this destructive line of thinking.** **
****[DONATE](http://links.wesleyfortexas.com/u/click?_t=fd5d6e7aa0334e7ebb096a39dc496016&_m=080194f66664408885a006b33547cc50&_e=H8-wYX9UsvuR-C6QGN3EIqfmLHtOh3VjtBYFTNoe6K_hviEPIYiDKDmXykCntZudCbtbK7gTrOoWd1bvj1az8vF1s8PqwLNDxiFKaCSzFj2cQ_cQw3GdkF0yI4ioBJAdTPs6wFnuXiMqh42ELG0yifAeUwFkuxXBgM9xckD6u1hBmHJZUqBUW0jKCNLYqzsVmcPBGk4TyiXlgkz2oN9IPfe8i7AOLVmNRJyr9sGW0G1JIJQ49gY07nPfTKJG0Hex4vAqZyGlkcNPo0njW-tprY3BznM0TJ1vxe9w_YioWPwCvV-f1OGJXgYxk5BuU_mM3PvH9zvFoeVHzSApvrsuPITF67mJ3EtVcjkHM31e-GSzLh1PmOwCvSd9SmUq-Ag7I5pxguQ5NVfxPJgfxtXLr5Cc4ynyy31bWUmkll2iKpklvTu6N3p0E__YCqMsAhJ7)****
---

name: Gov. Mike Huckabee
subject: We need you, right now.
body:
| | Peter, “Right now” is when the 2020 elections will be won or lost. And one of our long-standing supporters understands that which is why she is offering the first-ever $100,000 “Matching Check Challenge” for My Faith Votes. **[That means when you donate today, she will give the same amount... instantly DOUBLING your investment in our work](https://www3.myfaithvotes.org/p190801apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190801apl01nd&n=1).** **BUT... The Challenge has a two-week deadline. Whatever we do not raise before the deadline we do not get.** And now, when every bit makes a difference, I don’t want to leave even a single dollar behind. It will take your participation if we’re to raise the full $100,000. Don’t think for a minute that your contribution doesn’t matter. I am here to tell you that it does—more than you know. **You know as well as I do what the consequences of apathy at the polls are: devastating moral decay, declining religious freedom, immoral national debt, and the erosion of traditional family values.** And in a time when socialism – the deadliest ideology history has ever known – is becoming more-and-more popular here in America, it is possible that the radical Left will take control of our government. There is a long list of candidates running for President who represent extreme, progressive views that will make us a godless nation. They have already amassed millions-and-millions of campaign dollars. They are recruiting thousands of people for their grassroots teams. And they are building massive infrastructures to get out the vote. **I believe God has called on us – you, me, the members of My Faith Votes – to raise-up an army of tens of millions of Christians who will advance heaven on earth by living out their faith and voting from a biblically informed worldview.** **[How do we do it? With our proven plan and with you as our partner](https://www3.myfaithvotes.org/p190801apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190801apl01nd&n=2).** We worked so hard in the previous two elections. And it paid-off:
* Leading up to the last presidential election, My Faith Votes engaged 1.5 million people per day in battleground states. As a result, 85 percent of Christians we reached turned out to vote their values—well above the national average of 55 percent.

* Our impact increased in 2018, as My Faith Votes equipped Christians with an online voter guide covering all 70,000 elections across the country, from U.S. Senate down to local school boards.

We are committed to helping every Christian bring the influence of their faith to every election. And your investment today can help execute this proven battle plan again in 2019 and 2020. **[The friend who has invested her own $100,000 to make this possible has given us a rare and tremendous opportunity to maximize our effectiveness](https://www3.myfaithvotes.org/p190801apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190801apl01nd&n=3).** If ever there were a time to contribute, it is TODAY when your contribution means DOUBLE the funding for our nationwide communications, Church outreach, Christian college campus canvassing, and get-out-the Christian vote initiatives. That’s what it will take to protect the sanctity of human life; uphold marriage between a man and woman; ensure our religious freedoms; protect our borders; shrink the size of government; choose our own healthcare; preserve our Constitution; and honor the fundamental founding principles upon which this nation was established. So please help us secure every penny of the $100,000 in our “Matching Check Challenge” and double your impact on your family, your faith, your future in the face of the Left’s tyranny. [](https://www3.myfaithvotes.org/p190801apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190801apl01nd&n=4) **United. We Stand.**

Gov. Mike Huckabee
Honorary National Chairman
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190801apl01nd&n=5)
---
* * *

10940 S Parker, 640, Parker, CO 80134

name: Teresa Tomlinson
subject: Not in our community. Not in our State. Not in our Country.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TERESA TOMLINSON FOR SENATE
body:
Friend,

This week in Columbus the Temple Israel synagogue was vandalized by an extremist white nationalist hate group.

This group grew from the violently racist, anti-Semitic gathering in Charlottesville, Virginia in 2017. Its members are NOT “very fine people,” as the president once stated. They are the worst of us.

This vile attack on a Columbus synagogue perpetrated by a white supremacist hate group, with members across Georgia, is only the latest in a disturbing trend of increased anti-Semitism and hate crimes across America since 2016.

But while we are disturbed by this heinous act we will not hide in fear and we will not be deterred from our fight for justice and equality.

I am, like Rabbi Beth Schwartz, thankful that no one was hurt and that the local police responded quickly, and, yet, I pray for the end of such hatred and ignorance. We must exhibit strength by standing in solidarity with the members of Columbus’ Temple Israel and all Jewish Americans.

Make no mistake about it - the hate-crime vandals are not patriots as they propose. The members of the Columbus Jewish community are our neighbors and patriots. I stand with them and against hate. All Georgians should join us in declaring that we are intolerant of this intolerance.

Not in our community. Not in our state. Not in our country.

With you in the fight,
Teresa

body:
| | |
---
| | [](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcecc/3eb3e2fc/5b9995d0/744bf66c/548683031/VEsH/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://www.julianforthefuture.com?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=20190801_em_post-debate-win&source=20190801_em_post-debate-win")

| | Detroit was great to us. **[CNN](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcecc/3eb3e2fc/5b9995d0/744bf66b/548683031/VEsE/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://www.cnn.com/2019/07/31/politics/who-won-democratic-debate--who-won-democratic-debate-night-2/index.html?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=2+-+CNN&utm_campaign=20190801_em_post-debate-win&source=20190801_em_post-debate-win") and [The Washington Post](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcecc/3eb3e2fc/5b9995d0/744bf66a/548683031/VEsF/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2019/08/01/winners-losers-night-two-cnn-debate/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=3+-+The+Washington+Post&utm_campaign=20190801_em_post-debate-win&source=20190801_em_post-debate-win") declared our campaign a winner. ** But it wasn't just my performance that won, it was my ideas. Before I even took the stage on Wednesday night, my immigration policy, calls for impeachment and support for police reform and reparations shaped the conversation in Tuesday's debate. It echoes something I said on stage--**When you elect me president, it 's because you want me to lead, not follow. ** I didn't get into this race to play it safe. I got in it because Americans deserve a bold vision for our country, rooted in solid policy. They have to believe that we can be better than we've ever been. It's the only way we will get people to show up and vote Donald Trump out of this White House. Thank you for your continued support,
Julian \-- **Catch up below on what people are saying.** [_CNN_ : Winners and losers from the second night of the CNN debate](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcecc/3eb3e2fc/5b9995d0/744bf669/548683031/VEsC/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://www.cnn.com/2019/07/31/politics/who-won-democratic-debate--who-won-democratic-debate-night-2/index.html?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=4+-+CNN+Winners+and+losers+from+the+second+n&utm_campaign=20190801_em_post-debate-win&source=20190801_em_post-debate-win") **Juli an Castro: **_Two times is a trend. And it's been two straight debates where the former San Antonio mayor has stood out -- in a good way. He probably had the line of the night, hitting back on Biden with this hammer: "It looks like one of us has learned the lessons of the past and one of us hasn't." Castro was forceful and effective on immigration and really stuck it to New York City Mayor Bill de Blasio on how to handle the officer who choked Eric Garner. The challenge for Castro now is to qualify for the September and October debates, which could be a heavy lift given his polling thus far. If Castro can make these next two debates, he could really make noise in the race._ [_The Washington Post:_ Winners and losers from Night 2 of the second Democratic debate](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcecc/3eb3e2fc/5b9995d0/744bf668/548683031/VEsD/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2019/08/01/winners-losers-night-two-cnn-debate/?utm_term=.901e6e4d939e&utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=5+-+The+Washington+Post+Winners+and+losers+f&utm_campaign=20190801_em_post-debate-win&source=20190801_em_post-debate-win") **Juli an Castro:** _Castro might have been the standout. He ranked second after the first debate when it came to who pleasantly surprised people the most, but he didn 't get really any polling bump out of it. He was strong again on Wednesday night -- when he got the chance. At one point, he got in one of the most crowd-pleasing digs at Biden: "Mr. Vice President, it looks like one of us has learned the lessons of the past, and one of us hasn't," he said while talking about immigration. ". . . What we need are politicians who actually have some guts on this issue."_ **Want to help us keep delivering our message to voters across the country?**[ DONATE >>](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcecc/3eb3e2fc/5b9995d0/744bf657/548683031/VEsA/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/julian-castro-2020?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=6+-++DONATE+gtgt&utm_campaign=20190801_em_post-debate-win&source=20190801_em_post-debate-win")

subject: Baltimore Murder Rate Worse than Honduras, El Salvador, Guatemala, Driving Asylum Surge
body:
"The Baltimore murder rate is higher than the three Central American nations driving the border surge by migrants seeking to flee crime..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c9f0a36498&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Baltimore Murder Rate Worse than Honduras, El Salvador, Guatemala, Driving Asylum Surge**

“The Baltimore murder rate is higher than the three Central American nations driving the border surge by migrants seeking to flee crime and murder back home,” Paul Bedard reports in the _Washington Examiner_.

“In an analysis of the murder rates done after President Trump criticized Baltimore Rep. Elijah Cummings over the weekend, Baltimore's was reported at 56 per 100,000. The city is on track for 340 murders. By comparison, said the Princeton Policy Advisors analysis, the murder rate in El Salvador was 50, in Guatemala it was 22 and Honduras was 38.”

The bottom line: “That Baltimore's murder rate is higher than the most dangerous countries' in Central America is frankly appalling on many levels, and as someone who grew up in Baltimore, I believe increased accountability is long overdue,” Princeton Policy President Steven Kopits said.

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3e35f1a254&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “The Trump administration announced plans to allow the importation of drugs from other countries, a major part of President Trump's agenda for lowering the cost of prescriptions,” Cassidy Morrison reports for the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f6e3837f23&e=3ee4985eea). “This is the next important step in the Administration’s work to end foreign freeloading and put American patients first,” Health and Human Services Secretary Alex Azar said yesterday.
---
|
---
| In [_The Daily Caller_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b2c2be5886&e=3ee4985eea), Surya Gunasekara and Mandy Gunasekara write that President Trump’s newly confirmed UN Ambassador, Kelly Knight Craft, is America’s best hope for saving the once-proud institution. For years, American tax dollars have been wasted allowing authoritarian regimes, including Iran, to hobnob in the Big Apple. “While member states willingly turn a blind eye to these fundamental problems, they continue to pass anti-Semitic resolutions denigrating the one true beacon of hope and key ally the U.S. has in the middle east — Israel.”
---
|
---
| “As capital comes back home to the U.S., we are seeing President Trump’s tax reform pay off bigly for our country,” former Reagan economist Art Laffer writes in [Fox Business](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2299990f28&e=3ee4985eea). “Not so long ago, companies viewed the U.S. as a poor choice for headquartering international business operations. This was because the U.S. had one of the highest corporate income tax rates in the world,” he says. No longer—and the results prove it.
---
|
---
| USMCA is a chance to modernize North America’s outdated trade agreement, “with higher standards, and better policies that will help workers and businesses be even more competitive in the 21st-century economy,” writes Jon Barela, CEO of the Borderplex Alliance, in the [_El Paso Herald-Post_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=58d2170f55&e=3ee4985eea). “The next step is congressional action to ratify this critical agreement. We need our U.S. Representatives and Senators in Congress to immediately declare their support and vote to ratify the accord soon.”
---
|

body:
Peter,

July is a notoriously difficult month for fundraising, and yet so many of you helped out. **We surpassed our goal and each day we 're assembling the strongest campaign possible for 2020.**

We're now entering the August district work period, and I'm traveling around Central Virginia hosting town halls, meeting with constituents, and listening to the needs of families like yours. [](https://act.myngp.com/el/2ypNX7HzH2X-6XZ2iEhkUvLGrY-yE_KvPLkpSOjIQ10=/8TWTX6S04LqrJ_JMb0vGNo5drTTsm1_JwtADt5OGYQY=?refcode=BLU-SPAN-20190801-1-EM&amount=25&amounts=5,10,20,50,100,250)
You can see some of the coverage here from the [_Culpeper Star-Exponent_](https://act.myngp.com/el/2ypNX7HzH2X-6XZ2iEhkUvLGrY-yE_KvPLkpSOjIQ10=/psjmWxHS9LYMjVEkOyXN5eDbg_Mi0d_2PTe6wod7k3o=).

When I was sworn in, I knew that Congress was facing many enormous challenges. We've made real progress on important issues, but have a lot of work to do ahead of us. I'm incredibly proud to represent this district and so appreciative of all of the support.
Thank you again,

\-- Abigail

---
| | Peter -- Mayor Pete Buttigieg just made history as the FIRST presidential candidate to announce support for DC statehood on the debate stage:- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - Peter -- Mayor Pete Buttigieg just made history as the FIRST presidential candidate to announce support for D.C. statehood on the debate stage: [](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d0706/72412298/433562f7/6102c347/565880223/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG1WdFlXbHNJSDE5IjoiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iLCJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG5wcGNDQjlmUT09IjoiIn0=/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/support-dc-statehood?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_190801_PeteStatehood_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_190801_PeteStatehood_U1_X1") Pete knows that to get a transformational agenda passed we also need structural change. That means granting voting rights to the hundreds of thousands of D.C. residents who currently **have no representation in Congress** \-- even though D.C. has a higher population than Wyoming and Vermont. **With a critical hearing on D.C. statehood coming up soon, we need you to join us in our call for D.C. statehood now.[Add your name to voice your support for FULL representation for D.C. residents in Congress >>](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d0706/72412298/433562f7/6102c346/565880223/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG1WdFlXbHNJSDE5IjoiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iLCJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG5wcGNDQjlmUT09IjoiIn0=/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/support-dc-statehood?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=2+-+Add+your+name+to+voice+your+support+for+&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_190801_PeteStatehood_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_190801_PeteStatehood_U1_X1")** | | | [Support D.C. statehood >>](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d0706/72412298/433562f7/6102c349/565880223/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG1WdFlXbHNJSDE5IjoiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iLCJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG5wcGNDQjlmUT09IjoiIn0=/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/support-dc-statehood?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=3+-+Support+DC+statehood+gtgt&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_190801_PeteStatehood_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_190801_PeteStatehood_U1_X1")
---
Republican politicians are terrified of another Democratic state, because they know that could be what FINALLY takes all of Mitch McConnell's power away.

subject: What a week
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hickenlooper 2020
body:
This campaign is ready to hit the ground running once again. | | | [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771709/691c9bd/6e81c2df/11a4557c/814956221/VEsH/?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_template")
---
| Friend,

This campaign has had quite the week.

Thanks to your support, I reached 2% in a national poll, bringing me one step closer to qualifying for the September debates. Then, just days later, I stepped onto the Democratic debate stage for the second time this summer. And despite sharing that stage with nine other candidates, our pragmatic, progressive vision for America stood out.

Today it's back to work for me; this campaign is ready to hit the ground running once again. But it's important to me to take the time now to express my gratitude for each and every one of you.

**Thank you, thank you, thank you.** It is your continued support that has made us a real contender in this race. I can't wait to see what we accomplish together.

Giddy up,
John [](https://secure.hickenlooper.?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_x_20190801_JH_20190801-x-JH-thankyou-all_all_contribbutton&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=&zip=34223&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

But what I'm looking forward to most this weekend is listening to Kentuckians tell me about the issues that matter to them.

**While I 'm focused on speaking directly with the people of Kentucky, my finance director Meg continues to remind me that running a statewide campaign takes money. name: Tim Kaine
subject: Flipping the Virginia Senate - Step 1
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
| | | _I'm going to send you a series of emails about some of our incredible candidates for Virginia's General Assembly who are on the ballot this November. These are really strong leaders who are the key to flipping the legislature to Democratic control and securing our ability to make real progress in Richmond._

**This is the first in that series, and it's featuring three women all running for Senate in my hometown of Richmond:[ Ghazala Hashmi](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1766/3e0906/789d4d44/3e30eb4d/4118248808/VEsH/?g=_j7eKVH8x8VayGMy1IzDoYA "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-ghazala-hashmi?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=1+-+Ghazala+Hashmi&utm_campaign=20190801_3_FR_TK_FTS1&source=20190801_3_FR_TK_FTS1") in Senate District 10, [Amanda Pohl ](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1766/3e0906/789d4d44/3e30eb4c/4118248808/VEsE/?g=_j7eKVH8x8VayGMy1IzDoYA "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-amanda-pohl?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=2+-+Amanda+Pohl+&utm_campaign=20190801_3_FR_TK_FTS1&source=20190801_3_FR_TK_FTS1")in Senate District 11, and [Debra Rodman](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1766/3e0906/789d4d44/3e30eb43/4118248808/VEsF/?g=_j7eKVH8x8VayGMy1IzDoYA "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-debra-rodman?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=3+-+Debra+Rodman&utm_campaign=20190801_3_FR_TK_FTS1&source=20190801_3_FR_TK_FTS1") in Senate District 12.**
---

Derek:
There's a whole lot going on every single day in this country. Robert Mueller's testimony in Congressional hearings, this administration's policy of intentional cruelty at the border, and the President's continued attempts to dismantle affordable health care, just to name a few things. It can be tough to figure out how to keep up with everything, let alone take action that moves us forward. But those aren't the only things happening. There are also moments, events, and people that inspire us and give us hope for the future.

One of the things I'm most inspired by right now is the amazing people who have stepped up to run for office across school districts and cities and states all around our country. These people are looking at all of the news and saying "enough is enough." They're ready to get involved in their communities and find some solutions.

_**I want to tell you today about three of those inspiring people in my own community, Richmond:**_

**Ghazala Hashmi, running for State Senate in District 10:**
Dr. Ghazala Hashmi has been working for more than 25 years in Virginia's college and university system. She came to the U.S. from India 50 years ago, and saw first-hand how important a strong community is in bringing people together. As an educator and the mom of daughters who were educated in Chesterfield County Public Schools, Ghazala is passionate about the way a strong public education system can open doors and improve lives, and her public service is rooted in integrity and social justice. If elected, Ghazala would be the first Muslim-American woman to serve in the Virginia State Senate. **I'm so pleased to support her candidacy for State Senate, and I hope you'll join me.[ Make sure she's ready to win in November by making a donation to her campaign here.](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1766/3e0906/789d4d44/3e30eb42/4118248808/VEsC/?g=_j7eKVH8x8VayGMy1IzDoYA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-ghazala-hashmi?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=4+-+Make+sure+shes+ready+to+win+in+November+&utm_campaign=20190801_3_FR_TK_FTS1&source=20190801_3_FR_TK_FTS1")**
******[**DONATE TO GHAZALA HASHIMI**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1766/3e0906/789d4d44/3e30eb41/4118248808/VEsD/?g=_j7eKVH8x8VayGMy1IzDoYA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-ghazala-hashmi?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=5+-+DONATE+TO+GHAZALA+HASHIMI&utm_campaign=20190801_3_FR_TK_FTS1&source=20190801_3_FR_TK_FTS1")******
---

**Amanda Pohl, running for State Senate in District 11:**
Amanda Pohl is a hospital chaplain, church deacon, adjunct professor of Social Work, nonprofit professional, and mom who's ready to fight for affordable and accessible health care, sustainable infrastructure, and equality for every Virginian. Amanda was the first in her family to graduate from a 4-year college -- after spending her early years in subsidized housing -- and has lived in Chesterfield with her husband and two daughters for more than 15 years. I know that she'll represent Virginia families with the same dedication, conscientiousness, and compassion that she's shown through her entire career, and **I'm glad to support her run for State Senate.[ Please join me and chip in to her campaign here.](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1766/3e0906/789d4d44/3e30eb40/4118248808/VEsA/?g=_j7eKVH8x8VayGMy1IzDoYA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-amanda-pohl?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=6+-+Please+join+me+and+chip+in+to+her+campai&utm_campaign=20190801_3_FR_TK_FTS1&source=20190801_3_FR_TK_FTS1")**
******[**DONATE TO AMANDA POHL**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1766/3e0906/789d4d44/3e30eb47/4118248808/VEsB/?g=_j7eKVH8x8VayGMy1IzDoYA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-amanda-pohl?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=7+-+DONATE+TO+AMANDA+POHL&utm_campaign=20190801_3_FR_TK_FTS1&source=20190801_3_FR_TK_FTS1")******
---

**Debra Rodman, running for State Senate in District 12:**
Debra Rodman is a member of the Virginia House of Delegates, a Fulbright scholar, a teacher, and a community advocate. As a State Delegate, Debra played a critical role in expanding Medicaid in the Commonwealth, bringing health care access to more than 400,000 Virginians. She serves on the Agriculture Committee, where she pushes for adopting cleaner energy sources and fights for clean air and water. She's also a believer in lifting up public schools, and delivered a 5% raise for Virginia public school teachers this year. **Keeping her strong voice in the General Assembly will help Debra continue to do good for Virginians, and[ I hope you'll join this fight by contributing to her campaign.](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1766/3e0906/789d4d44/3e30eb46/4118248808/VEsO/?g=_j7eKVH8x8VayGMy1IzDoYA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-debra-rodman?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=8+-+I+hope+youll+join+this+fight+by+contribu&utm_campaign=20190801_3_FR_TK_FTS1&source=20190801_3_FR_TK_FTS1")**
******[**DONATE TO DEBRA RODMAN**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1766/3e0906/789d4d44/3e30eb45/4118248808/VEsP/?g=_j7eKVH8x8VayGMy1IzDoYA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-debra-rodman?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=9+-+DONATE+TO+DEBRA+RODMAN&utm_campaign=20190801_3_FR_TK_FTS1&source=20190801_3_FR_TK_FTS1")******
---

Thanks for taking the time to read about these candidates. I hope you feel as inspired as I do by their hard work and commitment to public service. There are less than 100 days until the November election, which means NOW is the time to jump in and support our great Democrats who are running tough races.

I appreciate your continued support,

-Tim Kaine

There are also moments, events, and people that inspire us and give us hope for the future. One of the things I'm most inspired by right now is the amazing people who have stepped up to run for office across school districts and cities and states all around our country. These people are looking at all of the news and saying "enough is enough." They're ready to get involved in their communities and find some solutions. I want to tell you today about three of those inspiring people in my own community, Richmond: Ghazala Hashmi, running for State Senate in District 10: Dr. Ghazala Hashmi has been working for more than 25 years in Virginia's college and university system. She came to the U.S. from India 50 years ago, and saw first-hand how important a strong community is in bringing people together. As an educator and the mom of daughters who were educated in Chesterfield County Public Schools, Ghazala is passionate about the way a strong public education system can open doors and improve lives, and her public service is rooted in integrity and social justice. If elected, Ghazala would be the first Muslim-American woman to serve in the Virginia State Senate. I'm so pleased to support her candidacy for State Senate, and I hope you'll join me. "" **** ""** Amanda Pohl, running for State Senate in District 11: Amanda Pohl is a hospital chaplain, church deacon, adjunct professor of Social Work, nonprofit professional, and mom who's ready to fight for affordable and accessible health care, sustainable infrastructure, and equality for every Virginian. Amanda was the first in her family to graduate from a 4-year college -- after spending her early years in subsidized housing -- and has lived in Chesterfield with her husband and two daughters for more than 15 years. I know that she'll represent Virginia families with the same dedication, conscientiousness, and compassion that she's shown through her entire career, and I'm glad to support her run for State Senate. "" ** ""** Debra Rodman, running for State Senate in District 12: Debra Rodman is a member of the Virginia House of Delegates, a Fulbright scholar, a teacher, and a community advocate. As a State Delegate, Debra played a critical role in expanding Medicaid in the Commonwealth, bringing health care access to more than 400,000 Virginians. She serves on the Agriculture Committee, where she pushes for adopting cleaner energy sources and fights for clean air and water. She's also a believer in lifting up public schools, and delivered a 5% raise for Virginia public school teachers this year. Keeping her strong voice in the General Assembly will help Debra continue to do good for Virginians, and "" ** ""** Thanks for taking the time to read about these candidates. I hope you feel as inspired as I do by their hard work and commitment to public service. There are less than 100 days until the November election, which means NOW is the time to jump in and support our great Democrats who are running tough races. I appreciate your continued support, -Tim Kaine | "" "" ""

subject: Christian Science Monitor: "Why America remains a beacon of hope for Liberian refugee"

body:
#
Team,
Maddie Collins, Wilmot's wife and chair of Women for Wilmot, recently talked about her story of coming to America, her family, and what gives her hope with the Christian Science Monitor. We wanted to make sure you had the opportunity to read and share this powerful story. More below.
**[contribute](https://act.wilmotcollins.?g=ENsuPVNw52hc6i6rfY8XtkA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190801&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**
**Why America remains a beacon of hope for Liberian refugee**
By Christa Case Bryant, The Christian Science Monitor

When the war got especially hard - when bullets littered the streets and food was scarce in the Liberian capital of Monrovia - Maddie Collins would think of the mountains and streams of Montana.

There, on a ranch outside Helena, lived a family that had welcomed her with open arms as a high school exchange student. All during the Liberian civil war, she carried her Helena High School diploma with her in a secret zippered pocket. Those were harrowing times - sucking condensed milk out of a can for sustenance, having her home invaded in the middle of the night, taking refuge in the operating room of a hospital during a military raid.

Back in Montana, her honorary American family stood ready to host her again. On her first attempt, the visa official denied her application.

But her host parents lobbied their congressional delegation and, with the help of a local nursing school in Helena where Ms. Collins planned to enroll, they succeeded in securing her a student visa. Then, on the eve of her departure, she got two hard pieces of news. The United States would not allow Wilmot, her new husband, to accompany her. And she learned she was pregnant.

Ms. Collins went anyway, moved back in with the Nachtsheims, and enrolled in nursing school. When she went into labor, Mr. Collins was thousands of miles away.

"I remember thinking, 'I wish Wilmot could see her right now. I don't think he'd ever see her in a more beautiful light,'" said Jaymie Sheldahl, Ms. Collins' American "sister," in an interview last year.

After giving birth, Ms. Collins placed her daughter in Jaymie's arms. "Her name," she said, "is Jaymie Louette."

"It was the family and sense of community I had in Helena, Montana, that gave me hope that home was waiting for Wilmot and me," says Ms. Collins. "But getting there was the hardest part, and it was the part I left up to God."

Little Jaymie was nearly 2 years old before her dad finally arrived at the Helena airport. Today, the Collinses are pillars of Helena, the capital of the least black state in America. Two years ago, Wilmot Collins - encouraged by their son, Bliss - became the first African American elected mayor in the history of Montana's statehood.

Now Mr. Collins is running for the U.S. Senate. Jaymie is serving in the U.S. Navy. And Ms. Collins, who works at the local VA hospital, is less than a month away from completing her doctorate of nursing practice as a geriatric nurse practitioner.

If there is one theme running through the Collinses' work, their military service, and their countless volunteer activities, it is a desire to give back to the community and the country that gave them a second chance.

"While Liberia will always be a part of me and who I am today, America symbolized a sense of renewal for me," says Ms. Collins. "Because even after everything Wilmot and I went through together, it was America that showed us hard work and patience will pay off, and if you stay true to yourself in this country and treat others with love and decency, your past doesn't solely define what your future will be."

In those early days in Helena, she would drive past the National Guard Armory on her way to nursing school, and her heart would start pounding - a vestige of the trauma she had experienced during the war. But she decided to confront that fear, she explained in an interview last year. "I just joined to get over my fear - and to give back," said Ms. Collins, who has risen to the rank of captain in the Army Nurse Corps.
She has also worked for Habitat for Humanity and helped with the local food share. Her family recently helped a Cuban family settle here, just as others had done for them. Now the Cubans have also become very involved in the community and are teaching salsa dancing.

As a Ph.D. student, she has been pursuing a project to help the city's homeless population. "My heart is in helping the marginalized of society - people who don't have the means to speak for themselves or are too busy struggling to stay alive," she says.

It has not all been easy in Helena. Her family has faced racist threats and slights over the years, and her husband's mayoral election was not warmly received by all. Montana has a strong white supremacist movement, and Mr. Collins' advocacy for Montana to reopen its doors to refugees faced resistance.

While she believes diversity contributes to America's strength, she says it's been a challenge for various groups to understand each other across those differences. That will only get harder, she says, if polarization and division are embedded in every aspect of society. "We are vulnerable to losing our [status as a] symbol of hope and freedom in the world's eyes," says Ms. Collins.

But she adds, "I've seen America rebuild itself in times of great difficulties historically, and since I have arrived in this country," such as after 9/11 or the Great Recession. "Our collective vision for an America that accepts people of all backgrounds is what I believe will help us overcome this challenge sooner rather than later. That's what gives me hope."
Thank you for reading and for your support!
Team Wilmot

body:
The Vice President joined Advisor to the President Ivanka Trump for a roundtable with Operation New Hope. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5ef5133cff&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • August 1, 2019
---
| | |
# **Video: Vice President Pence and Ivanka Trump promote job opportunities for all**
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f99f8d30d2&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **“We want America** to be the worst place in the world to commit a serious crime, but we also want America to be the best place in the world, once you’ve done your time, to get a second chance,” Vice President Mike Pence said in Jacksonville, Florida, last Friday.

**The Vice President** joined Advisor to the President Ivanka Trump for a roundtable with Operation New Hope, which for 20 years has been bringing opportunities for a fresh start to people who made the wrong choices early in their lives.

**“The reality** is that more than 50 percent of the people who leave prison are re-incarcerated within five years,” Vice President Pence said. But President Donald J. Trump’s historic criminal justice reform “now is creating new pathways for educational coursework, vocational training, faith-based programs in our federal prison system. And it’s been supported broadly by law enforcement across this country,” he added.

**In Florida** and across America, the U.S. economy is firing on all cylinders. That means more jobs are available to help former inmates adjust to life on the outside and become productive members of their communities. It also means more opportunities exist to help all working- and middle-class Americans thrive, regardless of background.

**“Worker wages are growing** much faster than previously reported,” _The Wall Street Journal_ editorial board wrote this week. Compensation rose 4.5 percent in 2017 and 5 percent last year. In June, wages and salaries grew at an annual rate of 5.5 percent—still an incredible 4.1 percent after accounting for inflation.

**“In sum** , Americans are earning more and relying less on government,” the editors wrote.

**In Jacksonville** , Vice President Pence and Ms. Trump toured Miller Electric, a company that embodies what this Trump Economy is all about. In total, Miller Electric has added more than 300 new jobs since early last year. It has also grown its apprenticeship program, which now totals more than 500 people across the country.

**American workers** and students “of every age, income, and background should have the chance to pursue their dreams and succeed and thrive according to their abilities and their ambitions,” Vice President Pence told the company’s employees. When you look at those growing wages, “they’re actually rising at the fastest rate for hardworking, blue-collar Americans. The forgotten men and women of America are forgotten no more.”

**The White House’s** Pledge to America’s Workers is doubling down on that promise. The initiative launched last summer, aiming to expand workforce training and reskilling opportunities in the United States. Last week, as the Pledge celebrated its one-year anniversary, Ivanka Trump announced the tally: more than _12 million_ job training opportunities pledged thus far.

[_The Wall Street Journal_ : “The 99% Get a Bigger Raise”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=95c3f72da9&e=3ee4985eea)

**Matthew Charles:**[Thanks to President Trump, “I got my second chance.”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=77ad1d9fdd&e=3ee4985eea)
